message:
feat(stdlib): add strtonum (OpenBSD bounded decimal parser) (bd-7i0gb)

OpenBSD strtonum(nptr, minval, maxval, errstr) — parse a decimal
integer into [minval, maxval] with categorized error reporting via
*errstr. Used by sshd, pf, bgpd, and other OpenBSD utilities for
safer numeric parsing than strtoll(3): no errno fiddling, no
overflow surprises, range check baked in, NO hex/octal/binary
prefix recognition (this is the whole point of strtonum).

* core::stdlib::strtonum::parse returns Result<i64, StrtonumError>
  with Invalid / TooSmall / TooLarge / InvalidRange variants.
  Detects overflow during accumulation rather than wrapping; treats
  trailing non-digit garbage as Invalid (stricter than strtoll).
* stdlib_abi::strtonum shim handles NUL-terminated input, NULL
  errstr/nptr guards, and writes one of OpenBSD's three canonical
  static error strings ("invalid", "too small", "too large")
  through *errstr — matching the byte content downstream callers
  in OpenBSD utilities check against.

25 core unit tests (RFC-style happy path, range/sign/overflow,
trailing-garbage, hex-prefix rejected, double-sign rejected,
i64::MIN/MAX boundary) + 12 abi integration tests (NULL errstr/
nptr safety, error-string byte equality, full i64 range,
overflow-as-too-large). Clippy-clean, fmt-clean. All builds via
rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
